include(FetchContent)

#----------------------------------------
# On ARM64, create an x86intrin.h stub backed by the bundled sse2neon.h so
# bonsai/hll code compiles using ARM NEON instead of hitting unsupported x86 intrinsics.
#----------------------------------------
if(APPLE AND CMAKE_SYSTEM_PROCESSOR MATCHES "arm64|aarch64")
    set(SSE2NEON_HEADER "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/extern/sse2neon.h")
    # Create a stub directory that intercepts #include "x86intrin.h" / #include <x86intrin.h>
    set(SSE2NEON_STUB_DIR "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/arch_stubs")
    file(MAKE_DIRECTORY "${SSE2NEON_STUB_DIR}")
    # Write a comprehensive stub that:
    # 1. Defines __SSE__/__SSE2__ so SIMD code paths are selected
    # 2. Includes sse2neon.h for NEON implementations of SSE2 intrinsics
    # 3. Replaces macro-defined intrinsics with real functions (for decop function-pointer use)
    # 4. Provides stubs for AVX-512 masked ops referenced in bonsai's declare_all macro
    file(WRITE "${SSE2NEON_STUB_DIR}/x86intrin.h" [=[
/* x86intrin.h ARM64 stub — generated by thirdparty/CMakeLists.txt */
#pragma once
#ifndef __SSE__
#  define __SSE__  1
#endif
#ifndef __SSE2__
#  define __SSE2__ 1
#endif
/* SSE4.1 flag: lets bonsai's vec.h define _mm_mullo_epi64 via its SSE4.1 fallback,
 * using intrinsics that sse2neon.h provides (hadd_epi32, mullo_epi32, etc.) */
#ifndef __SSE4_1__
#  define __SSE4_1__ 1
#endif
]=])
    file(APPEND "${SSE2NEON_STUB_DIR}/x86intrin.h" "#include \"${SSE2NEON_HEADER}\"\n")
    file(APPEND "${SSE2NEON_STUB_DIR}/x86intrin.h" [=[

/* sse2neon.h uses push_macro/pop_macro on FORCE_INLINE, so it is undefined after
 * the include ends.  Redefine it here so our inline wrappers below can use it.
 */
#ifndef FORCE_INLINE
#  define FORCE_INLINE static inline __attribute__((always_inline))
#endif

/* sse2neon.h defines srli_epi* as GCC statement-expression macros in C++ mode.
 * bonsai's decop() needs real function addresses.  Undef the macros and provide
 * FORCE_INLINE wrappers using NEON directly.
 */
#ifdef _mm_srli_epi16
#undef _mm_srli_epi16
FORCE_INLINE __m128i _mm_srli_epi16(__m128i a, int imm) {
    if ((unsigned)imm > 15) return _mm_setzero_si128();
    return vreinterpretq_m128i_u16(vshlq_u16(vreinterpretq_u16_m128i(a), vdupq_n_s16(-(int16_t)imm)));
}
#endif
#ifdef _mm_srli_epi32
#undef _mm_srli_epi32
FORCE_INLINE __m128i _mm_srli_epi32(__m128i a, int imm) {
    if ((unsigned)imm > 31) return _mm_setzero_si128();
    return vreinterpretq_m128i_u32(vshlq_u32(vreinterpretq_u32_m128i(a), vdupq_n_s32(-(int32_t)imm)));
}
#endif
#ifdef _mm_srli_epi64
#undef _mm_srli_epi64
FORCE_INLINE __m128i _mm_srli_epi64(__m128i a, int imm) {
    if ((unsigned)imm > 63) return _mm_setzero_si128();
    return vreinterpretq_m128i_u64(vshlq_u64(vreinterpretq_u64_m128i(a), vdupq_n_s64(-(int64_t)imm)));
}
#endif
#ifdef _mm_srli_si128
#undef _mm_srli_si128
FORCE_INLINE __m128i _mm_srli_si128(__m128i a, int imm) {
    if ((unsigned)imm > 15) return _mm_setzero_si128();
    uint8_t tmp[32] = {};
    vst1q_u8(tmp, vreinterpretq_u8_m128i(a));
    return vreinterpretq_m128i_u8(vld1q_u8(tmp + imm));
}
#endif

/* AVX-512 masked float/double ops — referenced by bonsai's declare_all(ps/pd,) in the SSE2
 * path but never actually called when HAS_AVX_512=0.  Provide stubs so decop() compiles.
 */
#ifndef __mmask8
typedef unsigned char  __mmask8;
#endif
#ifndef __mmask16
typedef unsigned short __mmask16;
#endif
FORCE_INLINE __m128  _mm_mask_and_ps(__m128 s,__mmask8,__m128 a,__m128 b)    {(void)s;return vreinterpretq_m128_u32(vandq_u32(vreinterpretq_u32_m128(a),vreinterpretq_u32_m128(b)));}
FORCE_INLINE __m128  _mm_maskz_and_ps(__mmask8,__m128 a,__m128 b)            {return vreinterpretq_m128_u32(vandq_u32(vreinterpretq_u32_m128(a),vreinterpretq_u32_m128(b)));}
FORCE_INLINE __m128  _mm_mask_andnot_ps(__m128 s,__mmask8,__m128 a,__m128 b) {(void)s;return _mm_andnot_ps(a,b);}
FORCE_INLINE __m128  _mm_maskz_andnot_ps(__mmask8,__m128 a,__m128 b)         {return _mm_andnot_ps(a,b);}
FORCE_INLINE __m128d _mm_mask_and_pd(__m128d s,__mmask8,__m128d a,__m128d b)    {(void)s;return vreinterpretq_m128d_u64(vandq_u64(vreinterpretq_u64_m128d(a),vreinterpretq_u64_m128d(b)));}
FORCE_INLINE __m128d _mm_maskz_and_pd(__mmask8,__m128d a,__m128d b)            {return vreinterpretq_m128d_u64(vandq_u64(vreinterpretq_u64_m128d(a),vreinterpretq_u64_m128d(b)));}
FORCE_INLINE __m128d _mm_mask_andnot_pd(__m128d s,__mmask8,__m128d a,__m128d b){(void)s;return _mm_andnot_pd(a,b);}
FORCE_INLINE __m128d _mm_maskz_andnot_pd(__mmask8,__m128d a,__m128d b)         {return _mm_andnot_pd(a,b);}
]=])
    # Stub emmintrin.h / immintrin.h so code like xxhash.h (#include <emmintrin.h>
    # under __SSE2__) gets our redirect instead of the x86-only system header.
    foreach(_stub emmintrin.h immintrin.h)
        file(WRITE "${SSE2NEON_STUB_DIR}/${_stub}"
"/* ARM64 stub: redirect ${_stub} to sse2neon (already included via x86intrin.h) */\n\
#pragma once\n\
#ifndef __SSE__\n\
#  define __SSE__  1\n\
#endif\n\
#ifndef __SSE2__\n\
#  define __SSE2__ 1\n\
#endif\n\
#ifndef __SSE4_1__\n\
#  define __SSE4_1__ 1\n\
#endif\n\
#include \"${SSE2NEON_HEADER}\"\n")
    endforeach()
    # Expose stub dir globally for targets that include bonsai headers
    set(SSE2NEON_STUB_DIR "${SSE2NEON_STUB_DIR}" CACHE INTERNAL "")
endif()

#----------------------------------------
# Add malloc_count
#----------------------------------------
FetchContent_Declare(
  malloc_count
  GIT_REPOSITORY https://github.com/bingmann/malloc_count
  )
  
FetchContent_GetProperties(malloc_count)
if(NOT malloc_count_POPULATED)
  FetchContent_Populate(malloc_count)

  add_library(malloc_count OBJECT ${malloc_count_SOURCE_DIR}/malloc_count.c ${malloc_count_SOURCE_DIR}/malloc_count.h)
  target_link_libraries(malloc_count dl)
  target_include_directories(malloc_count PUBLIC "${malloc_count_SOURCE_DIR}")

  add_library(memprofile OBJECT ${malloc_count_SOURCE_DIR}/memprofile.h)
  target_include_directories(memprofile PUBLIC "${malloc_count_SOURCE_DIR}")
endif()

#----------------------------------------
# Add klib
#----------------------------------------
FetchContent_Declare(
  klib
  GIT_REPOSITORY https://github.com/oma219/klib
)

FetchContent_GetProperties(klib)
if(NOT klib_POPULATED)
  FetchContent_Populate(klib)
  
  # add_subdirectory(${klib_SOURCE_DIR} ${klib_BINARY_DIR} EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL)
  add_library(klib INTERFACE)

  target_include_directories(klib INTERFACE ${klib_SOURCE_DIR})
endif()

#----------------------------------------
# Add zlib
#----------------------------------------

FetchContent_Declare(
  zlib
  GIT_REPOSITORY https://github.com/madler/zlib.git
)

FetchContent_GetProperties(zlib)
if(NOT zlib_POPULATED)
  FetchContent_Populate(zlib)
  add_library(zlib INTERFACE)
  include_directories(${zlib_SOURCE_DIR})
  #target_include_directories(zlib INTERFACE ${zlib_SOURCE_DIR})
endif()

#----------------------------------------
# Add bonsai
#----------------------------------------
FetchContent_Declare(
  bonsai
  GIT_REPOSITORY https://github.com/dnbaker/bonsai.git
  GIT_TAG        5273b81a92
  UPDATE_DISCONNECTED TRUE  # prevent git stash/unstash from reverting source patches
)

FetchContent_GetProperties(bonsai)
if(NOT bonsai_POPULATED)
  FetchContent_Populate(bonsai)
  # Apply scalar fallback patches to bonsai's vec.h for non-SIMD platforms.
  # On ARM64/macOS, sse2neon.h provides SIMD via ARM NEON, so these fallbacks
  # are only needed as a safety net for other non-x86 platforms.
  set(_bonsai_vec "${bonsai_SOURCE_DIR}/hll/include/vec/vec.h")
  execute_process(
    COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND}
      -DVEC_FILE=${_bonsai_vec}
      -P "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/CMakeModules/PatchBonsaiVec.cmake"
  )
  # Fix bonsai's iterator_traits specialisations: 'reference_type' → 'reference'
  # (C++17 std::iterator_traits requires 'reference', not 'reference_type').
  foreach(_cq
      "${bonsai_SOURCE_DIR}/circularqueue/cq.h"
      "${bonsai_SOURCE_DIR}/hll/include/circularqueue/cq.h")
    if(EXISTS "${_cq}")
      file(READ "${_cq}" _cq_content)
      if("${_cq_content}" MATCHES "using reference_type = T &")
        string(REPLACE "using reference_type = T &" "using reference = T &" _cq_content "${_cq_content}")
        file(WRITE "${_cq}" "${_cq_content}")
        message(STATUS "Patched iterator_traits::reference in ${_cq}")
      endif()
    endif()
  endforeach()
  add_library(bonsai INTERFACE)
endif()

#----------------------------------------
# Add Big-BWT
#----------------------------------------
FetchContent_Declare(
  bigbwt
  GIT_REPOSITORY https://github.com/oma219/Big-BWT.git
  )
  
FetchContent_GetProperties(bigbwt)
if(NOT bigbwt_POPULATED)
  FetchContent_Populate(bigbwt)

  # "using namespace __gnu_cxx" is unused and fails on Apple Clang (libc++ has no __gnu_cxx)
  file(READ ${bigbwt_SOURCE_DIR}/newscan.cpp _newscan)
  string(REPLACE "using namespace __gnu_cxx;\n" "" _newscan "${_newscan}")
  file(WRITE ${bigbwt_SOURCE_DIR}/newscan.cpp "${_newscan}")

  # strerror_r returns int on BSD/macOS (POSIX), not char* (GNU); patch xerrors.c to handle both
  file(READ ${bigbwt_SOURCE_DIR}/xerrors.c _xerrors)
  string(REPLACE
    "  char *errmsg = strerror_r(en, buf, Buflen);"
    "#ifdef __APPLE__\n  strerror_r(en, buf, Buflen);\n  char *errmsg = buf;\n#else\n  char *errmsg = strerror_r(en, buf, Buflen);\n#endif"
    _xerrors "${_xerrors}")
  file(WRITE ${bigbwt_SOURCE_DIR}/xerrors.c "${_xerrors}")

  # pfbwt.cpp: remove unused __gnu_cxx and add const to operator< so libc++ heap/sort accepts it
  file(READ ${bigbwt_SOURCE_DIR}/pfbwt.cpp _pfbwt)
  string(REPLACE "using namespace __gnu_cxx;\n" "" _pfbwt "${_pfbwt}")
  string(REPLACE "  bool operator<(const SeqId& a);" "  bool operator<(const SeqId& a) const;" _pfbwt "${_pfbwt}")
  string(REPLACE "bool SeqId::operator<(const SeqId& a) {" "bool SeqId::operator<(const SeqId& a) const {" _pfbwt "${_pfbwt}")
  file(WRITE ${bigbwt_SOURCE_DIR}/pfbwt.cpp "${_pfbwt}")

  add_subdirectory(${bigbwt_SOURCE_DIR} ${bigbwt_BINARY_DIR})

  # add_library(gsacak OBJECT ${bigbwt_SOURCE_DIR}/gsa/gsacak.c ${bigbwt_SOURCE_DIR}/gsa/gsacak.h)
  # target_include_directories(gsacak PUBLIC "${bigbwt_SOURCE_DIR}/gsa")
  
  # add_library(malloc_count OBJECT ${bigbwt_SOURCE_DIR}/malloc_count.c ${bigbwt_SOURCE_DIR}/malloc_count.h)
  # target_link_libraries(malloc_count dl)
  # target_include_directories(malloc_count PUBLIC "${bigbwt_SOURCE_DIR}")
  
  endif()

#----------------------------------------
# Add gsacak
#----------------------------------------
FetchContent_Declare(
  gsacak
  GIT_REPOSITORY https://github.com/felipelouza/gsa-is.git
)

FetchContent_GetProperties(gsacak)
if(NOT gsacak_POPULATED)
    FetchContent_Populate(gsacak)
    add_library(gsacak OBJECT ${gsacak_SOURCE_DIR}/gsacak.c ${gsacak_SOURCE_DIR}/gsacak.h)
    target_include_directories(gsacak PUBLIC "${gsacak_SOURCE_DIR}")
    
    add_library(gsacak64 OBJECT ${gsacak_SOURCE_DIR}/gsacak.c ${gsacak_SOURCE_DIR}/gsacak.h)
    target_include_directories(gsacak64 PUBLIC "${gsacak_SOURCE_DIR}")
    target_compile_options(gsacak64 PUBLIC -DM64)
  # add_subdirectory(${gsacak_SOURCE_DIR} ${gsacak_BINARY_DIR} EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL)
endif()

#----------------------------------------
# Add sdsl
#----------------------------------------
set(CMAKE_C_FLAGS_OLD "${CMAKE_C_FLAGS}")
set(CMAKE_C_FLAGS "${CMAKE_C_FLAGS} -fPIC")
set(CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS_OLD "${CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS}")
set(CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS "${CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS} -fPIC")
FetchContent_Declare(
  sdsl
  # GIT_REPOSITORY https://github.com/elarielcl/sdsl-lite.git
  GIT_REPOSITORY https://github.com/simongog/sdsl-lite
)
# sdsl's CMakeLists.txt checks CMAKE_COMPILER_IS_CLANGXX which is not set for Apple Clang
# (compiler ID "AppleClang"). Without it, sdsl falls into the MSVC branch and adds
# -DMSVC_COMPILER, which pulls in <process.h> (a Windows header) and breaks compilation.
# Set the variable before FetchContent_MakeAvailable so sdsl's subdirectory inherits it.
if("${CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID}" STREQUAL "AppleClang")
  set(CMAKE_COMPILER_IS_CLANGXX ON)
endif()
FetchContent_MakeAvailable(sdsl)
unset(CMAKE_COMPILER_IS_CLANGXX)

# sdsl uses std::result_of which was removed in C++20; replace with invoke_result so that
# headers included from C++20 translation units (e.g. shaped_slp) compile correctly.
# Patch both the source tree and the build-dir copy (sdsl copies headers into the build dir).
foreach(_iter_path
    "${sdsl_SOURCE_DIR}/include/sdsl/iterators.hpp"
    "${sdsl_BINARY_DIR}/include/sdsl/iterators.hpp")
  if(EXISTS "${_iter_path}")
    file(READ "${_iter_path}" _iterators)
    string(REPLACE
      "typedef typename std::result_of<t_F(size_type)>::type         value_type;"
      "#if __cplusplus >= 201703L\n    typedef typename std::invoke_result<t_F, size_type>::type value_type;\n#else\n    typedef typename std::result_of<t_F(size_type)>::type value_type;\n#endif"
      _iterators "${_iterators}")
    file(WRITE "${_iter_path}" "${_iterators}")
  endif()
endforeach()

# sdsl bug: louds_tree::swap() references m_select1/m_select0 but members are m_bv_select1/m_bv_select0
# Patch both the source tree and the build-dir copy (sdsl copies headers into the build dir)
foreach(_louds_path
    "${sdsl_SOURCE_DIR}/include/sdsl/louds_tree.hpp"
    "${sdsl_BINARY_DIR}/include/sdsl/louds_tree.hpp")
  if(EXISTS "${_louds_path}")
    file(READ "${_louds_path}" _louds)
    string(REPLACE "tree.m_select1" "tree.m_bv_select1" _louds "${_louds}")
    string(REPLACE "tree.m_select0" "tree.m_bv_select0" _louds "${_louds}")
    file(WRITE "${_louds_path}" "${_louds}")
  endif()
endforeach()

if(NOT sdsl_POPULATED)
  FetchContent_Populate(sdsl)

  set(GENERATE_DOC OFF CACHE BOOL "Do not generate doxygen for sdsl-lite")
  add_subdirectory(${sdsl_SOURCE_DIR} ${sdsl_BINARY_DIR} EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL)
endif()

set(CMAKE_C_FLAGS "${CMAKE_C_FLAGS_OLD}")
set(CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS "${CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS_OLD}")

#----------------------------------------
# Add divsuffsort
#----------------------------------------
FetchContent_Declare(
  divsufsort
  GIT_REPOSITORY https://github.com/simongog/libdivsufsort.git
  GIT_TAG        2.0.1
)

FetchContent_GetProperties(divsufsort)
if(NOT divsufsort_POPULATED)
  FetchContent_Populate(divsufsort)

  set(BUILD_SHARED_LIBS OFF CACHE BOOL "Do not build a shared library for libdivsufsort")
  set(BUILD_EXAMPLES OFF CACHE BOOL "Do not build libdivsufsort example")
  set(BUILD_DIVSUFSORT64 ON CACHE BOOL "Build libdivsufsort in 64-bits mode")

  # sdsl bundles its own libdivsufsort; only add subdirectory if targets don't already exist.
  # Either way, expose the generated include dir (divsufsort.h is generated at build time)
  # so that r-index and other consumers can find it when linking against divsufsort.
  if(NOT TARGET divsufsort)
    add_subdirectory(${divsufsort_SOURCE_DIR} ${divsufsort_BINARY_DIR} EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL)
    target_include_directories(divsufsort PUBLIC "${divsufsort_BINARY_DIR}/include")
    target_include_directories(divsufsort64 PUBLIC "${divsufsort_BINARY_DIR}/include")
  else()
    # sdsl already defined the targets; its generated headers are in the sdsl build tree
    target_include_directories(divsufsort PUBLIC "${sdsl_BINARY_DIR}/external/libdivsufsort/include")
    target_include_directories(divsufsort64 PUBLIC "${sdsl_BINARY_DIR}/external/libdivsufsort/include")
  endif()
endif()

#----------------------------------------
# Add r-index
#----------------------------------------
FetchContent_Declare(
  r-index
  GIT_REPOSITORY https://github.com/maxrossi91/r-index.git
)

FetchContent_GetProperties(r-index)
if(NOT r-index_POPULATED)
  FetchContent_Populate(r-index)

  # ri-align.cpp passes a std::string directly to fprintf %s; Apple Clang treats this as an error
  file(READ ${r-index_SOURCE_DIR}/ri-align.cpp _rialign)
  string(REPLACE
    "fprintf(stderr, \"Error: unknown option %s\\n\", s);"
    "fprintf(stderr, \"Error: unknown option %s\\n\", s.c_str());"
    _rialign "${_rialign}")
  file(WRITE ${r-index_SOURCE_DIR}/ri-align.cpp "${_rialign}")

  add_subdirectory(${r-index_SOURCE_DIR} ${r-index_BINARY_DIR} )#EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL)
  add_library(ri INTERFACE)
  target_link_libraries(ri INTERFACE klib z)
  target_include_directories(ri INTERFACE ${r-index_SOURCE_DIR}/internal)
endif()

#----------------------------------------
# Add pfp-thresholds
#----------------------------------------
FetchContent_Declare(
  pfp_thresholds
  GIT_REPOSITORY https://github.com/oma219/pfp-thresholds.git
  GIT_TAG master
  )
  
FetchContent_GetProperties(pfp_thresholds)
if(NOT pfp_thresholds_POPULATED)
  FetchContent_Populate(pfp_thresholds)
  add_subdirectory(${pfp_thresholds_SOURCE_DIR} ${pfp_thresholds_BINARY_DIR})
  
endif()

#----------------------------------------
# Add bigrepair
#----------------------------------------
FetchContent_Declare(
  bigrepair
  GIT_REPOSITORY https://gitlab.com/omaryfekry/bigrepair.git
  )
  
FetchContent_GetProperties(bigrepair)
if(NOT bigrepair_POPULATED)
  FetchContent_Populate(bigrepair)
  # add_subdirectory(${bigrepair_SOURCE_DIR} ${bigrepair_BINARY_DIR})

  # Build only the targets spumoni needs; skip 'ctph' which fails to compile on
  # Apple Clang (strerror_r returns int on BSD/macOS, not char*)
  execute_process(COMMAND make CC=$ENV{CC} CXX=$ENV{CXX} procdic postproc
        RESULT_VARIABLE result
        WORKING_DIRECTORY ${bigrepair_SOURCE_DIR})
  if(result)
      message(FATAL_ERROR "CMake step for bigrepair failed: ${result}")
  endif()
  execute_process(COMMAND make CC=$ENV{CC} CXX=$ENV{CXX} -C largeb_repair
        RESULT_VARIABLE result
        WORKING_DIRECTORY ${bigrepair_SOURCE_DIR})
  if(result)
      message(FATAL_ERROR "CMake step for bigrepair largeb_repair failed: ${result}")
  endif()
  
endif()

#----------------------------------------
# Add ShapedSlp
#----------------------------------------
FetchContent_Declare(
  shaped_slp
  GIT_REPOSITORY https://github.com/koeppl/ShapedSlp.git
  GIT_TAG master
  UPDATE_DISCONNECTED TRUE  # prevent CMake from stashing/unstashing our patches on reconfigure
  )
  
FetchContent_GetProperties(shaped_slp)
if(NOT shaped_slp_POPULATED)
  FetchContent_Populate(shaped_slp)

  # sux/support/common.hpp unconditionally includes <x86intrin.h> which only exists on x86/x64.
  # Guard it so ARM64 (Apple Silicon) builds are not broken.
  set(_sux_common "${shaped_slp_SOURCE_DIR}/external/sux/sux/support/common.hpp")
  file(READ "${_sux_common}" _sux)
  string(REPLACE
    "#include <x86intrin.h>"
    "#if defined(__x86_64__) || defined(__i386__)\n#include <x86intrin.h>\n#endif"
    _sux "${_sux}")
  file(WRITE "${_sux_common}" "${_sux}")

  # RecSplit.hpp uses constexpr float math (sqrt/ceil/log/pow) which Apple Clang does not
  # support as constexpr even in C++20 (standardised only in C++26).
  # Fix: replace lower_aggr/upper_aggr with equivalent pure-integer expressions; remove
  # constexpr from the three functions that use float math; change the two static constexpr
  # member arrays to static inline const (C++17 runtime initialisation).
  set(_recsplit "${shaped_slp_SOURCE_DIR}/external/sux/sux/function/RecSplit.hpp")
  file(READ "${_recsplit}" _rc)
  # Integer equivalents: ceil(0.35*n + 0.5) == (35*n + 149)/100, ceil(0.21*n + 0.9) == (21*n + 189)/100
  string(REPLACE
    "static constexpr size_t lower_aggr = _leaf * max(2, ceil(0.35 * _leaf + 1. / 2));"
    "static constexpr size_t lower_aggr = _leaf * (size_t)max((int64_t)2, (int64_t)(35 * _leaf + 149) / 100);"
    _rc "${_rc}")
  string(REPLACE
    "static constexpr size_t upper_aggr = lower_aggr * (_leaf < 7 ? 2 : ceil(0.21 * _leaf + 9. / 10));"
    "static constexpr size_t upper_aggr = lower_aggr * (_leaf < 7 ? (size_t)2 : (21 * _leaf + 189) / 100);"
    _rc "${_rc}")
  # Remove constexpr from functions using float math
  string(REPLACE
    "template <size_t LEAF_SIZE> static constexpr void _fill_golomb_rice("
    "template <size_t LEAF_SIZE> static void _fill_golomb_rice("
    _rc "${_rc}")
  string(REPLACE
    "template <size_t LEAF_SIZE> static constexpr array<uint32_t, MAX_BUCKET_SIZE> fill_golomb_rice()"
    "template <size_t LEAF_SIZE> static array<uint32_t, MAX_BUCKET_SIZE> fill_golomb_rice()"
    _rc "${_rc}")
  string(REPLACE
    "static constexpr array<uint8_t, MAX_LEAF_SIZE> fill_bij_midstop()"
    "static array<uint8_t, MAX_LEAF_SIZE> fill_bij_midstop()"
    _rc "${_rc}")
  string(REPLACE
    "template <size_t LEAF_SIZE> static constexpr uint64_t split_golomb_b("
    "template <size_t LEAF_SIZE> static uint64_t split_golomb_b("
    _rc "${_rc}")
  # Change static constexpr member arrays to static inline const (C++17 runtime init)
  string(REPLACE
    "static constexpr array<uint32_t, MAX_BUCKET_SIZE> memo = fill_golomb_rice<LEAF_SIZE>();"
    "static inline const array<uint32_t, MAX_BUCKET_SIZE> memo = fill_golomb_rice<LEAF_SIZE>();"
    _rc "${_rc}")
  string(REPLACE
    "static constexpr array<uint8_t, MAX_LEAF_SIZE> bij_midstop = fill_bij_midstop();"
    "static inline const array<uint8_t, MAX_LEAF_SIZE> bij_midstop = fill_bij_midstop();"
    _rc "${_rc}")
  file(WRITE "${_recsplit}" "${_rc}")

  add_subdirectory(${shaped_slp_SOURCE_DIR} ${shaped_slp_BINARY_DIR})
  set(FOLCA_SOURCE_DIR ${shaped_slp_SOURCE_DIR}/folca)
  set(SUX_SOURCE_DIR ${shaped_slp_SOURCE_DIR}/external/sux/sux)
endif()
